tool_args.label: Flatbush Avenue Bus Priority
tool_args.raw_text: Flatbush Avenue Bus Priority - Brooklyn Community Board 14 Transportation Committee – January 5, 2023

payload.description: Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue, a critical cross-borough transportation corridor in Brooklyn. Part of the Transit Improvement Summit announced June 16, 2022 by Mayor Eric Adams and MTA Chair Janno Lieber.

tool_args.label: Flatbush Avenue, Tillary Street to Kings Plaza
tool_args.raw_text: Connects downtown Brooklyn to commercial hubs, cultural and educational institutions and medical care in three major sections: Tillary St to Empire Blvd, Empire Blvd to Nostrand Ave, Nostrand Ave to Kings Plaza

payload.description: Critical cross-borough transportation corridor connecting downtown Brooklyn to commercial hubs, cultural and educational institutions and medical care. Serves 118,000 average daily bus riders (2019) on 12 routes.

tool_args.label: B41
tool_args.raw_text: B41 is the primary route on Flatbush Ave operating local and limited service from Kings Plaza/Bergen Beach to downtown Brooklyn

payload.description: Primary route on Flatbush Ave operating local and limited service from Kings Plaza/Bergen Beach to downtown Brooklyn. Has 22,500 average daily riders.

tool_args.label: Flatbush Ave Vision Zero priority corridor

payload.claim_text: Flatbush Ave is a Vision Zero priority corridor for safety improvements
payload.description: Flatbush Avenue is a Vision Zero priority corridor with 10 Vision Zero priority intersections. 164 people have been killed or seriously injured on Flatbush Ave in the last 5 years.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 164 people have been killed or seriously injured on Flatbush Ave in the last 5 years. Flatbush Ave is a Vision Zero priority corridor for safety improvements
Flatbush Ave is a Vision Zero priority corridor, with 10 Vision Zero priority intersections

tool_args.label: Transit Improvement Summit announced June 2022

payload.description: Mayor Eric Adams and MTA Chair and CEO Janno Lieber announced a new collaborative effort to improve transit service, including completing 150 miles of new and enhanced bus lanes and busways over 4 years and launching planning for Flatbush Avenue bus priority

tool_args.label: Recent and planned projects on Flatbush Avenue

payload.claim_text: NYCDOT has implemented or is planning numerous safety and operational projects on Flatbush recently, including, but not limited to: Congested Corridor Project (2015), Flatbush Avenue and Atlantic Avenue Pedestrian Improvements (2018), Hillel Place Plaza (2018 temporary, 2026 capital), Protected Bike Lane along Prospect Park (2020), Flatbush Ave/Utica Ave/Ave S Pedestrian Improvements (2024), Flatbush Avenue Safety Improvements, Livingston St to Pacific St Capital Project (2026)

tool_args.label: Subway and LIRR connections on Flatbush Ave

payload.claim_text: Connections to B/D, N/Q/R, 2/3, 4/5, S subways; Long Island Railroad
payload.description: Buses on Flatbush Ave provide critical connections to numerous subway lines and the Long Island Railroad
